title: The 'Mini-Renaissance' in Marxist Educational Sociology: A critique
abstract: This paper argues that the recent 'mini-renaissance' in Marxist educational sociology as propounded in particular by The reason for this centres on the adoption of a universal internal relations social ontology, which results in the reduction of concrete social reality to the narrow abstraction of the omnipresent 'Capital Relation'. At the same time, such theorising remains conspicuously silent on the issue of the feasible alternatives to capitalism implied by the (albeit awed) explanatory critiques of such recent Marxist theorising within the sociology of education.
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
